body: PT Ikapharmindo Putramas Tbk (IKPM) saw a 67.27% increase in its share price to IDR 276 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 464.96 billion, while PT Mastersystem Infotama Tbk (MSTI) climbed 12.91% to IDR 1,530 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 4.80 trillion. Meanwhile, PT Kian Santang Muliatama Tbk reported a 20.83% decline in its share price to IDR 95 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 138.62 billion.
title: Stock performances of two out of three issuers that debuted on the Indonesia Stock Exchange in November improve last week.

body: PT Gajah Tunggal Tbk (GT) has secured a IDR 4.4 trillion syndicated loan facility from several banks, including PT Bank Central Asia Tbk, PT Bank Digital BCA, and others, to be used for paying off outstanding payables, including a USD 175 million senior secured note due in 2026. The new credit facility is expected to positively impact the company's financial performance, particularly by reducing foreign exchange risk, which could affect profitability.
title: PT Gajah Tunggal secures IDR 4.4 trillion syndicated loan from multiple banks to refinance outstanding debt.

body: Jakarta-based Formosa Ingredient Factory TBK (BOBA), a beverage ingredient manufacturer, will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 2.31 billion or IDR 2 per share, following approval from the board of commissioners on November 11, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 12.17 billion in the first nine months of 2024, with unrestricted retained earnings of IDR 31.22 trillion and total equity of IDR 155.13 billion.
title: Boba King's Parent Company to Distribute IDR 2.31 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders.

body: PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (ANTM) has successfully utilized 57,000 tons of bauxite washing waste to produce road construction materials, generating value from waste, and in 2023, the company produced 2.01 million wet metric tons of bauxite, with sales reaching 1.50 million wmt. ANTM's bauxite resources and reserves stood at 547.4 million wmt and 121.7 million wmt, respectively, in 2023, solidifying its position as a key player in the industry.
title: Nicolas D. Kanter, President Director of PT Aneka Tambang, wins Best CEO award for innovative approach to utilizing mining waste in various applications.

body: PT Jasa Marga Tbk (JSMR) President Director Subakti Syukur was awarded Best CEO at the Top CEO Awards event, recognizing his success in launching three green toll roads, which contributed to the company's net profit of IDR 6.79 trillion in 2023, with a compound annual growth rate of 57.26% over the past two years. JSMR's green toll road concept is part of its business sustainability roadmap through 2027, which has driven robust business growth and earned the company a spot in the Indeks52 main index, high growth, and big cap categories.
title: Jasa Marga's President Director Subakti Syukur Wins Best CEO Award for Launching Environmentally-Friendly Green Toll Roads.

body: PT Industri Jamu dan Farmasi Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO) has been recognized for its efforts in optimizing the use of locally-sourced raw materials, with its subsidiary PT Semarang Herbal Indoplant exporting 16 tons of patchouli essential oil to France in 2021. The company reported a net profit of IDR 950.65 billion in fiscal year 2023, with a compound annual growth rate of 3.31% over the past four years.
title: Sido Muncul's President Director David Hidayat Wins Best CEO Award for Optimizing Locally-Sourced Raw Materials.

body: Lani Darmawan, President Director of PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk (BNGA), was named Best CEO by Tempo-IDNFinancials for consistently delivering double-digit performance growth, with BNGA recording a net profit of IDR 6.47 trillion in 2023, up 28.41% year-on-year. The bank's strong financial performance has secured its position as one of Indonesia's largest banks, earning it a place in the Indeks52, with a compound annual growth rate of 38.78% in net profit.
title: Lani Darmawan, President Director of PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk, named Best CEO for delivering consistent double-digit performance growth.

body: Citibank N.A. Indonesia (Citi Indonesia) reported a 32% year-over-year increase in net profit at the end of September 2024, driven by operational expense efficiency, with a cost-to-income ratio improving to 41.9% from 59.8%. Despite this, the bank's credit distribution decreased to IDR 30.5 trillion in September 2024, down from IDR 42.84 trillion in 9M 2023, due to challenges in long-term investment and the transfer of retail banking assets to Bank UOB Indonesia.
title: Citi indonesia's ceo batara siantyri admitted that bank's credit quality third quarter 2024 performance was driven by strong revenue growth and solid expense management.
